text: TEAMFILM
There Is A Place in The Bottom Of The Scientists
It's the bread of destitution
My splats fly like new wind
There is no pollution
You with no words to hold
Hopes, questions, or solutions
Bedrock of a river that flow
No past, present, or future
We are men of nature
We are made from the earth
At the end of my 80
I'll return to earth
Just sand, just rock
Trap, land, rest, and silent
Only being, only breathing
We're just children of believers
Like fire and water, be strong with compassion
In the morning we're born everlasting
Like the grass by the sea
Bending with the wind which knocks it down
Time and again we remain dancing
Standing till the dawn of day
Carries us away as we sway through the faces
Of each generation
We leave our trace and then leave this station
Peace, France, fantasy, phase
No blame, untamed, unspoken
She walks through this space
On dry land that's cracked and broken
We came to taste the rain
We're just widows and orphans
Not afraid to feel the pain
Or to leave behind our notions
Bathe in showers, taste the tension
Hear the howl, climb the mountain
Kiss the cold that healed the frozen
Reaching dreams in this here dungeon
We are men of nature
We are made from the earth
At the end of my 80
I'll return to the earth
Just sand, just rock, dry land
Rest and silent
Only being, only breathing
